name: Stripe
security: Never expose STRIPE_SECRET_KEY in client components.
Always verify webhook signatures with the official SDK.
Treat webhooks as the source of truth for subscription state, not success_url.
Use restricted API keys (rk_) for server tasks that don't need full secret-key scope.
Set up a separate webhook secret per environment.
Never log raw payment payloads — Stripe IDs only.

patterns.name: Hosted Checkout
patterns.description: Create a Checkout Session server-side, redirect the user, handle completion via webhook.

patterns.name: Customer Portal
patterns.description: Generate a Billing Portal session so customers can self-serve subscription changes and invoices.

patterns.name: Webhook-driven sync
patterns.description: Listen to customer.subscription.* and invoice.* events and sync state into your own database.

patterns.name: Embedded Payment Element
patterns.description: Use the Payment Element for an in-app checkout when redirecting away from your domain isn't acceptable.
